title: Legg Calve Perthes Disease
extracted_facts: Perthes: idiopathic AVN of proximal femoral epiphysis in children. Lateral pillar classification prognostic. Containment limits deformity.

title: Olecranon Fractures - Trauma - Orthobullets
extracted_facts: Osteology: olecranon + coronoid form greater sigmoid (semilunar) notch articulating with trochlea for flexion-extension + stability.
Muscles: triceps inserts posterior proximal ulna (blends periosteum, radial n C7); anconeus lateral olecranon (radial C7).
ORIF: tension band (transverse no comminution) converts distraction to compression (K-wires engage ant cortex risk AIN/forearm rotation); dorsal plate for comminuted/Monteggia/obli
Excision + triceps advancement: salvage elderly <50% joint, osteoporotic, nonunion (decreased extension strength, instability risk if ligs missed).

title: Wrist Arthroscopy - Hand - Orthobullets
extracted_facts: Position: supine, elbow 90 flex, 10lb finger traction tower. Landmarks: Lister tubercle, scaphoid, lunate, DRUJ, ECU.
Radiocarpal portals: 3-4 (1cm distal Lister between EPL/EDC; primary viewing; risk EPL/EDC); 4-5 (ring MC line between EDC/EDM; TFCC view/instr; risk EDC/EDM); 6R (radial ECU; ulna
Midcarpal: MCR (1cm distal 3-4 radial middle MC between ECRB/EDC; SL/SC/STT); MCU (1cm distal 4-5; LC/LT/TH).

title: Elbow Arthroscopy - Indications and Approach
extracted_facts: Portals (standard): proximal anterolateral (1-2cm prox, 1cm ant to lat epicondyle - radial n risk decreases proximally), distal anterolateral (1-3cm distal 1cm ant - PIN, lateral antebrachial cutaneous), direct lateral/soft spot (olecranon-radial head-lat epicondyle triangle - safest for distension and posterior viewing), anteromedial (2cm ant 2cm distal to med epicondyle - median, medial antebrachial cutaneous), proximal anteromedial (2cm prox med epicondyle anterior to intermuscular septum - ulnar 7mm, median).
Landmarks: olecranon, medial and lateral epicondyles, radiocapitellar joint, ulnar nerve (palpate).
Technique: distend joint first (20-30ml) to displace NV structures; nick and spread technique for portals.
Risks: radial/PIN and lateral antebrachial cutaneous (anterolateral portals), ulnar (proximal anteromedial ~7mm), median (anteromedial).
structures_at_risk: Radial nerve / PIN (anterolateral portals)
Ulnar nerve (proximal anteromedial ~7mm from septum)
Median nerve (anteromedial)
Lateral antebrachial cutaneous nerve
landmarks: Olecranon tip
Medial/lateral epicondyles
Radiocapitellar joint line
Ulnar nerve course
retrieval_phrases: direct lateral soft spot elbow scope
elbow arthroscopy radial PIN ulnar risk

title: Plantar Fasciitis
extracted_facts: Plantar fascia 3 bands (central/medial/lateral); origin medial calcaneus tuberosity; windlass mechanism increases arch on toe-off. Release medial 1/3-2/3 (avoid complete: arch collapse, lateral column overload, dorsolateral pain). Baxter's nerve (1st branch lateral plantar) at risk anterior to medial tuberosity between QP/FDB; consider release if entrapment. Gastroc recession for tight Achilles. Risks: lateral plantar n, complete release arch issues, heel pad atrophy, rupture (post-steroid/athletes). Landmarks: medial calc tuberosity, abductor hallucis.
structures_at_risk: Lateral plantar nerve/Baxter's (anterior medial tuberosity QP/FDB)
Arch destabilization from complete release
landmarks: Medial calcaneal tuberosity
Abductor hallucis
retrieval_phrases: plantar fasciitis release medial 1/3-2/3 Baxter nerve

title: Quadriceps Tendon Rupture
extracted_facts: Quad tendon 2-4 layers (ant RF, middle VMO/VL, deep VI). Repair: midline to knee, transpatellar holes or anchors, locking nonabsorb sutures, retinaculum repair. Chronic: V-Y (Codivilla) or graft. Postop brace locked ext, progressive ROM. Layers key for partial vs complete.
structures_at_risk: Patella (transpatellar fracture risk)
Extensor mechanism integrity
landmarks: Patella superior pole
Quad tendon

title: Cervical Laminectomy (anterior strong; posterior limited)
extracted_facts: Anterior: 3 fascia (investing platysma/EJV, pretracheal/carotid thyroid vessels, prevertebral/sympathetic trunk). Landmarks angle mandible C2-3, carotid tubercle C6. Planes platysma VII, SCM XI lat, straps C1-3 med, longus colli segmental. Deep split longus/ALL, subperiost, retract lat, needle localize. Risks RLN 2.3% (right variable), symp chain longus C6 (Horner), carotid, retrophar hematoma. Posterior: midline subperiost lamina, flavum lateral to dura; lateral mass for fusion. C5 palsy risk with foraminotomy.
structures_at_risk: RLN 2.3%, sympathetic C6 Horner, carotid, cord/nerve roots, C5 palsy
landmarks: Angle mandible C2-3
Carotid tubercle C6
Spinous, facets, lateral mass
retrieval_phrases: anterior cervical fascia RLN sympathetic longus
posterior cervical laminectomy C5 palsy lateral mass

title: Periprosthetic Hip (builds on hip approaches 12116/1037)
extracted_facts: Builds on native hip: short ERs, sciatic/superior gluteal post; femoral n/v, LFCN ant/lat. Approaches posterior (Moore) or lateral (Hardinge) or anterior per fracture/prior approach. Protect abductors, sciatic, femoral NV; assess stem stability. Risks amplified by stem interface/prior scars.
structures_at_risk: Sciatic/superior gluteal (post)
Femoral NV, LFCN (ant/lat)
Abductors (lat)
landmarks: GT/LT
Femoral shaft
Prior approach
retrieval_phrases: periprosthetic hip fracture approach protect sciatic femoral

title: Metacarpal/Boxers Fracture
extracted_facts: Metacarpal neck boxers: apex dorsal from interossei. Acceptable angulation higher 4th/5th (40-50deg) vs 2nd/3rd (<10-20) due CMC mobility. Reduction Jahss (flex MCP, dorsal push). ORIF if irreducible/open/multiple/malrotation (scissoring). Dorsal/lat approach; protect extensors, dorsal sensory. Landmarks MC heads, extensor tendons.
structures_at_risk: Extensor tendons
Dorsal sensory branches
landmarks: MC heads/neck
CMC

title: SCFE Pinning
extracted_facts: SCFE: post/inf epiphysis slip on metaphysis. Southwick angle, Klein line. Pinning: perc, center-center AP/lat fluoro (avoid ant sup pin penetration/chondrolysis). Start lat prox femur, traj ant to post/inf. Risks AVN (retinacular vessels), chondrolysis, progression, hardware. Anatomy: femoral head blood supply (retinacular, med fem circ), physis, capsule.
structures_at_risk: Femoral head retinacular (AVN)
Articular cartilage (chondrolysis pin penetration)
landmarks: GT
Femoral neck (Klein)
Physis fluoro
retrieval_phrases: SCFE pinning center-center fluoro AVN chondrolysis
